title: Weighted Shortest Paths and Relaxation ┬Ę Execution Trace: Bellman Ford
summary: Choose safe routes across positive, negative, and unreachable weighted networks. Planned artifact: Add and verify the dsa2 bellman ford increment in a multi-algorithm route engine with path proofs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use repeated relaxation to find shortest paths and reachable negative-cycle evidence.
Run Dijkstra only under compatible nonnegative weights and stale-entry handling.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: dsa2-bellman-ford ŌĆö Use repeated relaxation to find shortest paths and reachable negative-cycle evidence.
def relax(distance: dict[str, float], previous: dict[str, str], left: str, right: str, weight: float) -> bool:
    candidate = distance[left] + weight
    if candidate >= distance.get(right, float("inf")): return False
    distance[right], previous[right] = candidate, left
    return True
